faculty.name: Kasso Okoudjou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research covers pure, applied, and numerical harmonic analysis, with focus on time-frequency analysis, wavelet analysis, frame theory, and analysis and differential equations on fractals and graphs. Also works on data science applications and mathematics education, particularly active learning and increasing underrepresented minority participation in undergraduate research.

faculty.name: James Adler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops efficient computational methods for complex fluids, plasma physics, electromagnetism, and other physical applications. Focuses on adaptive finite-element discretizations and multigrid solvers for nonlinear PDEs that model multi-scale physical systems, aiming to produce accurate, physics-preserving solutions with optimal computational efficiency.

faculty.name: Bruce Boghosian
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Applied Dynamical Systems and Applied Probability
faculty.summary: Research centers on applied dynamical systems, applied probability theory, kinetic theory, and agent-based modeling. Currently emphasizes mathematical models of economics and agent-based models of wealth distributions, exploring wealth concentration, mobility, and oligarchy. Secondary work involves lattice Boltzmann models, kinetic theory, and quantum computation. Currently on academic leave.

faculty.name: Corey Bregman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Geometric Group Theory and Low-Dimensional Topology
faculty.summary: Works on geometric group theory, low-dimensional topology, and CAT(0) spaces. Earned PhD from Rice University in 2017. Currently on academic leave.

faculty.name: Fulton Gonzalez
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Noncommutative Harmonic Analysis and Integral Geometry
faculty.summary: Focuses on noncommutative harmonic analysis, representations of Lie groups, integral geometry, and Radon transforms. Studies reconstructing functions from indirect data such as averages over curves or surfaces, with applications in medical imaging and sonar.

faculty.name: Boris Hasselblatt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on geometrically motivated hyperbolic dynamics within dynamical systems theory. Studies hyperbolic phenomena and geometrically motivated systems. Founding editor of the Journal of Modern Dynamics and founding Editor-in-Chief of Mathematics Research Reports.

faculty.name: Xiaozhe Hu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on the development, analysis, and implementation of numerical methods for solving partial differential equations (PDEs) and graph problems. Specializes in parallel multigrid and multilevel methods for large-scale coupled systems, with applications in multiphase flow in porous media, complex fluids, magnetohydrodynamics, and fluid-structure interaction. Also works on GPU and multicore CPU parallelization of solvers.

faculty.name: Misha Kilmer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Numerical Linear Algebra and Scientific Computing
faculty.summary: Research focuses on numerical linear and multilinear algebra, scientific computing, and image reconstruction and restoration. SIAM Fellow (2021). Served as Deputy Director of ICERM at Brown University (2021-2025). Holds joint appointment in Computer Science.

faculty.name: Robert Lemke-Oliver
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on analytic number theory, with a particular focus on equidistribution problems and elliptic curves. Studies L-functions, modular forms, the distribution of prime numbers, and rational solutions to elliptic curve equations. Currently on academic leave.

faculty.name: George McNinch
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure and representations of algebraic groups, with a particular focus on linear algebraic groups in the modular setting where the coefficient field has prime characteristic. Research areas include linear representations, complete reducibility, conjugacy classes, Levi decompositions, and related cohomological issues.

faculty.name: James Murphy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans pure math, computational math, and applications to remote sensing, image processing, and medical data analysis. Theoretical focus includes harmonic analysis, machine learning, and theoretical statistics, with attention to unsupervised learning, spectral graph methods, and eigenfunctions of operators on graphs. Develops efficient, scalable algorithms for unsupervised learning. Applied work includes hyperspectral imagery analysis and ECG signal analysis. Currently on academic leave.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Newman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Numerical Linear Algebra and Machine Learning
faculty.summary: Research focuses on multidimensional (multilinear) algebra, numerical linear algebra, machine learning, and deep neural networks. Works on tensor-based methods for data analysis including tensor neural networks, tensor-tensor products, and tensor decompositions for image compression, deblurring, and classification. Recipient of Air Force FY25 Young Investigator Program Award and 2025 SIAM CSE Best Paper Prize. 2025-2026 Merrin Faculty Fellow.

faculty.name: Samantha Petti
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans computational biology, algorithms, probability, fitness landscapes, evolution, protein structure, and statistical inference. Holds joint appointment in Computer Science.

faculty.name: Kim Ruane
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geometric group theory, focusing on a class of groups similar to Fuchsian groups but much more general. Her work connects geometry, group theory, and topology to distinguish geometric objects by their symmetry groups. Began work on knot theory applications to DNA. Currently on academic leave.

faculty.name: David Smyth
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on algebraic geometry, focusing on the application of geometric methods to understand solutions of systems of polynomial equations. Particularly interested in curves, singularities, and moduli theory, aiming to establish systematic constructions and classifications of geometric compactifications of the moduli space of algebraic curves.

faculty.name: Abiy Tasissa
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops scalable and robust algorithms at the intersection of applied mathematics, machine learning, and data science. Key areas include high-dimensional data analysis (matrix completion, compressive sensing), mathematical foundations of AI (interpretable machine learning, dictionary learning), and applied optimization (distance geometry, large-scale kernel methods, algorithm unrolling).

faculty.name: Montserrat Teixidor I Bigas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on algebraic curves and moduli spaces. Studies vector bundles on curves, which appear in physics through Gromov-Witten invariants and have provided beautiful proofs to long-standing conjectures as well as striking counterexamples.

faculty.name: Genevieve Walsh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Geometric topologist who studies groups acting on spaces, hyperbolic manifolds and orbifolds, low-dimensional topology, and group actions. Her work is deeply influenced by 3-dimensional hyperbolic manifolds and orbifolds, and has addressed topics including hyperbolic 2-bridge knot complements, the virtually Haken conjecture, commensurability of knot complements, and right-angled Coxeter groups.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Mina Aganagic
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Aganagic's work sits at the intersection of mathematics and theoretical physics, using techniques from string theory and mirror symmetry to address problems in geometry and topology. Her research explores knot categorization, quantum Langlands correspondences, and topological vertex theory.

faculty.name: Suncica Canic
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Canic's research focuses on mathematical modeling and analysis of partial differential equations arising in fluid dynamics, particularly fluid-structure interaction problems with applications to cardiovascular mechanics, hemodynamics, and medical device design.

faculty.name: Jennifer Chayes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chayes's research applies tools from probability and graph theory to problems in machine learning and complex networks, with a focus on mathematical structures like graphons for modeling large-scale systems. She holds joint appointments in EECS, Information, and Statistics.

faculty.name: Steven N. Evans
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Evans' research spans stochastic processes and their applications to mathematical biology, including phylogenetics, population genetics, and evolutionary dynamics. His work develops probabilistic models for biological systems with rigorous mathematical foundations.

faculty.name: Ming Gu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gu develops fast and stable algorithms for large-scale linear algebra problems, including matrix factorizations, eigenvalue computations, and structured linear systems, spanning both theoretical foundations and practical applications in scientific computing and data analysis.

faculty.name: Venkatesan Guruswami
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Guruswami's research lies at the intersection of computer science and mathematics, focusing on error-correcting codes, algebraic methods for computation, and approximate optimization. He also serves as Director of the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing.

faculty.name: Olga Holtz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Holtz's work lies at the intersection of numerical linear algebra, matrix theory, and combinatorics, focusing on communication costs and computational complexity of algorithms. She investigates structured matrices, root localization of polynomials, and communication-efficient matrix multiplication.

faculty.name: Michael J. Lindsey
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Lindsey's research develops numerical methods and mathematical theory for computational physics problems, with emphasis on electronic structure calculations, quantum many-body systems, and tensor network methods bridging applied mathematics and computational quantum science.

faculty.name: Lin Lin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lin develops mathematical and computational methods for quantum mechanical systems, spanning numerical algorithms for quantum chemistry (Kohn-Sham DFT) and quantum computing algorithms including Hamiltonian simulation, phase estimation, and ground-state preparation.

faculty.name: Per-Olof Persson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Persson's research focuses on computational mathematics and scientific computing, particularly unstructured mesh generation, high-order discontinuous Galerkin methods, and their applications to computational fluid dynamics, fluid-structure interaction, and wave propagation problems.

faculty.name: Fraydoun Rezakhanlou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Rezakhanlou's work sits at the intersection of probability theory and PDEs, focusing on stochastic models and conservation laws. His research spans scalar conservation laws with random initial conditions, stochastic Navier-Stokes, particle systems, and multi-agent reinforcement learning.

faculty.name: James A. Sethian
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Sethian pioneered level set and fast marching methods for solving PDEs on irregular domains with moving interfaces, with applications spanning semiconductor manufacturing, seismic imaging, fluid dynamics, and tumor growth simulation.

faculty.name: Chris Shannon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Shannon applies rigorous mathematical tools including transversality theory, Lipschitz functions, and equilibrium analysis to foundational questions in economic theory, examining subjective beliefs, market stability, preference evolution, and strategic behavior.

faculty.name: Nikhil Srivastava
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Srivastava's work lies at the intersection of mathematics and theoretical computer science, focusing on random matrix theory and the geometry of polynomials. He co-resolved the Kadison-Singer problem and developed the theory of interlacing families, constructing bipartite Ramanujan graphs of all degrees.

faculty.name: Franziska Weber
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Weber's research centers on the theoretical and computational study of nonlinear partial differential equations, combining techniques from numerical analysis and applied mathematics. She is also affiliated with Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory.

faculty.name: Jon Wilkening
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Wilkening develops numerical methods and computational algorithms to solve PDEs, with applications ranging from fluid dynamics (water waves, swimming sheets) to materials science and micromagnetics, combining mathematical analysis with high-performance computing.

faculty.name: Mariusz Wodzicki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Wodzicki's research spans noncommutative geometry, cyclic homology, and algebraic K-theory, developing algebraic tools that bridge pure and applied mathematics, including work on determinants in infinite dimensions and zeta-regularized products.

faculty.name: Maciej Zworski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Zworski's research centers on semiclassical analysis and its applications to mathematical physics, particularly scattering theory and quantum chaos. He investigates resonances, quantum decay rates, and spectral properties of operators using microlocal analysis techniques.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Ian Agol
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Agol's research focuses on 3-manifold topology and hyperbolic geometry. He is best known for proving the virtual Haken conjecture (2013), establishing fundamental properties about surface subgroups in hyperbolic 3-manifolds, with ongoing work in low-dimensional topology, geometric group theory, and knot theory.

faculty.name: Richard Bamler
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Bamler's research centers on Ricci flow and geometric analysis, studying the evolution of metrics on manifolds. His work extends Perelman's breakthroughs on the Poincare and geometrization conjectures, with focus on regularity theory, singularity formation, and the structure of Ricci limit spaces.

faculty.name: Richard E. Borcherds
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Fields Medalist (1998) whose research spans vertex operator algebras, automorphic forms, and the mathematical structures underlying conformal field theory and string theory. He proved the monstrous moonshine conjecture, revealing deep connections between the monster group and modular forms.

faculty.name: Ben Davison
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.summary: Davison's research bridges enumerative geometry and geometric representation theory, applying geometric counting problems to advance understanding of algebraic structures such as Lie algebras and their representations. He works on cohomological Donaldson-Thomas theory and sheaf counting on Calabi-Yau threefolds.

faculty.name: David Eisenbud
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Eisenbud's research focuses on the algebraic structures underlying geometric objects, particularly syzygies, free resolutions, and sheaf theory, often using computational methods. He has made significant contributions to Boij-Soderberg theory and the study of varieties of minimal degree.

faculty.name: Edward Frenkel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Frenkel's work bridges representation theory, algebraic geometry, and mathematical physics, focusing on the geometric Langlands correspondence, vertex algebras, and integrable systems. His research explores deep connections between quantum field theory and the Langlands program.

faculty.name: Alexander Givental
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Givental's work lies at the intersection of symplectic geometry and mathematical physics, with foundational contributions to Gromov-Witten theory, quantum cohomology, and quantum K-theory. He pioneered the symplectic geometry of Frobenius structures and integrable hierarchies related to mirror symmetry.

faculty.name: Gabriel Goldberg
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Goldberg's research explores the hierarchy of large cardinal axioms and the internal structure of set-theoretic models, seeking to understand the limits of mathematical consistency and the nature of infinity through advanced set-theoretic methods including inner model theory and infinite combinatorics.

faculty.name: Vadim Gorin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Gorin studies exactly solvable probabilistic models at the intersection of probability and algebra, connecting random matrix theory with asymptotic representation theory. His work investigates the large-scale limiting behavior of random structures arising from algebraic and combinatorial systems.

faculty.name: Mark D. Haiman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Haiman's research in algebraic combinatorics focuses on symmetric functions, Macdonald polynomials, and diagonal harmonic spaces. He proved the n! conjecture and Macdonald positivity conjecture, establishing deep connections between combinatorics, representation theory, and algebraic geometry.

faculty.name: Alan Hammond
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Hammond applies modern probabilistic methods to study statistical mechanics rigorously, investigating models such as last-passage percolation, random polymers, and interacting particle systems through the lens of mathematical probability theory.

faculty.name: Michael Hutchings
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Hutchings' research centers on symplectic and contact geometry in low dimensions, pioneering embedded contact homology (ECH) as a tool to study phenomena such as the Weinstein conjecture, the Arnold chord conjecture, and quantitative aspects of symplectic embeddings using pseudoholomorphic curves and Floer theory.

faculty.name: Svetlana Jitomirskaya
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Jitomirskaya's research sits at the intersection of mathematical physics and dynamical systems, with focus on the spectral theory of quasiperiodic Schrodinger operators. Her work has deep connections to quantum mechanics and the Anderson localization phenomenon in disordered systems.

faculty.name: John Lott
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lott's work centers on differential geometry with focus on Ricci curvature and its interactions with optimal transport theory. His research extends curvature concepts to metric-measure spaces and includes substantial contributions to Ricci flow, including co-authoring influential expositions on Perelman's work.

faculty.name: Andrew Marks
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Marks' research program centers on descriptive set theory and its interplay with computability theory, combinatorics, ergodic theory, and operator algebras, investigating the structural and algorithmic properties of definable equivalence relations.

faculty.name: Amir Mohammadi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Mohammadi's research lies at the intersection of dynamical systems, geometry, and algebra, studying how group actions and ergodic theory illuminate the structure of discrete subgroups within Lie groups, connecting pure mathematical analysis with geometric and algebraic structures.

faculty.name: David Nadler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Nadler applies techniques from symplectic geometry to problems in representation theory, with work spanning Hecke categories, microlocal sheaf theory, derived loop spaces, and Lagrangian skeleta, reflecting a deeply geometric approach to algebraic and topological questions.

faculty.name: Martin Olsson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Olsson's research in algebraic and arithmetic geometry addresses deep structural questions involving schemes, stacks, and cohomological methods with arithmetic applications. His work spans K3 surfaces, abelian varieties, moduli spaces, log geometry, and Brauer groups.

faculty.name: Thomas Scanlon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Scanlon applies model theory to problems in number theory and algebraic geometry, bridging logical methods with deep questions in arithmetic geometry. His work addresses the Andre-Oort conjecture, polynomial dynamical systems, and Drinfeld modules through the lens of mathematical logic.

faculty.name: Vera Serganova
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Serganova's research focuses on representation theory, particularly the structure and representations of Lie superalgebras and supergroups. Her work develops fundamental tools for understanding supersymmetry in mathematics, including character formulas and Kazhdan-Lusztig theory for Lie superalgebras.

faculty.name: Sug Woo Shin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Shin's work lies at the intersection of number theory and automorphic forms, focusing on the Langlands program including the stable trace formula, p-adic geometry (Scholze's diamonds), and arithmetic aspects of Shimura varieties. His research investigates affine Deligne-Lusztig varieties and deformation spaces of p-divisible groups.

faculty.name: Yunqing Tang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Tang's research lies at the intersection of arithmetic geometry and number theory, exploring deep connections between algebraic structures and number-theoretic phenomena, with work on supersingular primes for abelian varieties and related arithmetic questions.

faculty.name: Daniel Tataru
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Tataru's research focuses on well-posedness, regularity, and long-time behavior of nonlinear dispersive and wave equations arising in mathematical physics. He investigates critical problems including fluid flows, general relativity on black hole backgrounds, and water waves, developing novel parametrices and energy estimates.

faculty.name: Constantin Teleman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Teleman's research lies at the intersection of geometry, topology, and algebra, using methods from quantum field theory and Lie theory to study moduli spaces, loop groups, and the geometric Langlands correspondence. His work connects mathematical physics (topological field theories, mirror symmetry) with pure mathematics.

faculty.name: Dan-Virgil Voiculescu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Voiculescu is the founder of free probability theory, which he developed to study operator algebras. His research bridges noncommutative geometry, random matrix theory, and von Neumann algebras through free entropy and limit laws for random matrices.

faculty.name: Katrin Wehrheim
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Wehrheim's research centers on symplectic geometry and polyfold theory, developing a rigorous analytic foundation for the study of pseudoholomorphic curves and Gromov-Witten invariants. Her polyfold framework provides a unified approach to regularization problems in symplectic topology and gauge theory.

faculty.name: Ruixiang Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Zhang's research sits at the intersection of analysis and number theory, applying techniques from harmonic analysis to problems in analytic number theory and additive combinatorics, exploring structural properties of numbers and functions through Fourier analysis and combinatorial methods.

faculty.name: Maciej Zworski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Zworski's research centers on semiclassical analysis and its applications to mathematical physics, particularly scattering theory and quantum chaos. He investigates resonances, quantum decay rates, and spectral properties of operators arising from physical systems using microlocal analysis techniques.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Numerical Linear Algebra and Scientific Computing
faculty.summary: Research in numerical linear algebra with emphasis on theory, algorithm development, and analysis for large-scale scientific computing problems.

faculty.name: Joseph Biello
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in applied mathematics with focus on multiscale asymptotics for PDEs, atmospheric sciences, and geophysical fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Rishidev Chaudhuri
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computational neuroscience and mathematical biology, using machine learning and dynamical systems approaches to study neural computation.

faculty.name: Raissa D'Souza
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in network theory, statistical physics, and computational science, studying complex systems including cascading failures, explosive percolation, and evolutionary dynamics.

faculty.name: Jesus De Loera
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in discrete mathematics with emphasis on algorithms in algebra and geometry, combinatorial optimization, and computational discrete geometry.

faculty.name: Albert Fannjiang
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Martin Fraas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in mathematical physics and quantum information theory, studying topological phases of matter and quantum many-body systems.

faculty.name: Abi Gopal
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Niels Gronbech-Jensen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in numerical analysis for molecular modeling and computational molecular/statistical mechanics, developing algorithms for biomolecular simulations.

faculty.name: Robert Guy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in mathematical biology, studying Newtonian and non-Newtonian fluid dynamics, immersed boundary methods, and biomechanics.

faculty.name: John Hunter
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.summary: Research in nonlinear wave propagation, continuum mechanics, and singular perturbation methods with applications to fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Sameer Iyer
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.summary: Research in partial differential equations and fluid mechanics, with emphasis on boundary layer theory and stability analysis.

faculty.name: Matthias Koeppe

faculty.name: Anuj Kumar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in turbulence, partial differential equations, optimization, and computational methods for fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Greg Kuperberg

faculty.name: Timothy Lewis

faculty.name: Bruno Nachtergaele
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Naoki Saito

faculty.name: Steve Shkoller

faculty.summary: Research in hyperbolic partial differential equations, moving free-boundary and interface problems, and mathematical fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Thomas Strohmer

faculty.name: Mariel Vazquez

faculty.name: Alexander Wein

faculty.name: Helen Dahlke

faculty.summary: Experimental research on surface-groundwater interaction, vadose zone transport, stochastic hydrology, and water resources management, including managed aquifer recharge.

faculty.name: Xiaoli Dong

faculty.name: Alvar Escriva-Bou

faculty.name: Alexander Forrest

faculty.name: Thomas Harter

faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor and Chair of the Hydrologic Sciences Graduate Group. Research on groundwater and vadose zone processes at the nexus of the agricultural landscape, non-point source pollution, and sustainable groundwater management.

faculty.name: Jonathan Herman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on water resources planning under uncertainty, multi-objective optimization, and system dynamics modeling for water management decisions.

faculty.name: Peter Hernes

faculty.name: William Horwath

faculty.name: Katrina Jessoe

faculty.summary: Research on design and evaluation of pricing and conservation policies in the water and electricity sectors.

faculty.name: Yufang Jin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in remote sensing of terrestrial ecosystems, crop monitoring, ecohydrology, and climate-vegetation-human interactions including fire disturbances.

faculty.name: Isaya Kisekka
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Irrigation Engineering and Agricultural Water Management
faculty.summary: Research on precision irrigation management, optimization of water and nutrient use efficiency, agrohydrology, crop modeling, and machine learning applications in agricultural water management.

faculty.name: John Largier
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in coastal oceanography, studying water motion and transport, estuarine dynamics, larval dispersal, and coastal water quality.

faculty.name: Mark Lubell
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Center for Environmental Policy and Behavior. Research on human behavior and governance institutions in solving collective action problems related to watershed management and agricultural best management practices.

faculty.name: Erwan Monier
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on climate modeling and uncertainty quantification, climate impact assessment, Energy-Water-Land dynamics, and interactions between climate change, air quality, and health.

faculty.name: Veronica Morales
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on micro-scale physico-chemical processes controlling transport and fate of colloids and nanoparticle contaminants in groundwater, and biochar engineering for water treatment.

faculty.name: Holly Oldroyd
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on environmental turbulent flows in rivers, lakes, and the atmosphere, including water-land-atmosphere interactions, evapotranspiration, and greenhouse gas transport.

faculty.name: Pramod Pandey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on engineering solutions for controlling microbial pathogens from animal waste and bacteria transport from farms to ambient water.

faculty.name: Gregory Pasternack
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on managing and restoring hydrogeomorphic processes for enhanced ecosystem functioning, river restoration and management, and geomorphic characterization of human impacts on watersheds.

faculty.name: Kyaw Tha Paw U
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in biometeorology and micrometeorology, studying evapotranspiration, turbulence, aerosol physics, and boundary layer meteorology.

faculty.name: Nicholas Pinter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Roy J. Shlemon Chair in Applied Geosciences. Research on earth-surface processes, fluvial geomorphology, flood hydrology, flood hazards, and natural-hazards public policy.

faculty.name: Brett Poulin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Trace Metal Contaminants in Surface Water
faculty.summary: Research on fate and transport of trace metal contaminants (especially mercury) in surface water, organic geochemistry, carbon cycling, using advanced X-ray techniques.

faculty.name: Steven Sadro
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on physical, chemical, and biological limnology of high-elevation lakes and streams, coupled lake-watershed processes, and climate change impacts on aquatic ecosystems.

faculty.name: Samuel Sandoval Solis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Robert M. Hagan Endowed Chair. Research on integrated water resources management, environmental flows, transboundary water basins, and water conservation strategies.

faculty.name: Tyler Scott
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Institutional Complexity and Water Resource Governance
faculty.summary: Chair of the Environmental Policy and Management Graduate Group. Research on institutional complexity affecting water resource behaviors, stakeholder input in infrastructure decision-making, and agent-based modeling.

faculty.name: Kosana Suvočarev
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in biometeorology with focus on land-atmosphere interactions and agricultural applications.

faculty.name: Daniele Zaccaria
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on agricultural water management, irrigation of fruit and nut trees, and water productivity in relation to environmental and natural resources.

requirements.admission_model: General GRE not required; GRE Subject exam indicated on central page

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Jun Allard
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical models of cell mechanics, studying how cells generate and respond to forces during actin-based crawling, internal transport, and cytoskeleton dynamics using nonlinear PDEs, stochastic simulation, and Bayesian statistics.

faculty.name: Kenneth Ascher
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in algebraic geometry and number theory, contributing to the Algebra and Number Theory group at UCI which spans coding theory, cryptography, elliptic curves, abelian varieties, and varieties over finite fields.

faculty.name: Vladimir Baranovsky
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in algebra and number theory, including algebraic geometry, noncommutative geometry, and related areas within the department's Algebra and Number Theory group.

faculty.name: Paul Carter
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans applied dynamical systems, mathematical and computational biology, and nonlinear partial differential equations.

faculty.name: Long Chen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on finite element methods, multiscale modeling and simulation, numerical analysis and scientific computing, and machine learning and data science.

faculty.name: Michael C. Cranston
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probability theory and stochastic processes, contributing to the Probability research group at UCI.

faculty.name: Christopher Davis
faculty.title: Professor of Teaching
faculty.summary: Research in coding theory and cryptography within the Algebra and Number Theory group; also serves as Vice Chair of Undergraduate Affairs.

faculty.name: German A. Enciso Ruiz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops generic modules for biochemical reactions and investigates signal transduction pathways tied to EGF receptors, applying computational modeling to understand complex biological signaling systems.

faculty.name: Asaf Ferber
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probability and combinatorics, contributing to the Probability research group at UCI.

faculty.name: Aleksandr Figotin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in mathematical physics spanning electromagnetic theory in linear and nonlinear complex media, statistical mechanics, and solid state physics including random and quasi-periodic Schrodinger operators.

faculty.name: Alexandra Florea
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in number theory, contributing to the Algebra and Number Theory group which studies L-functions, elliptic curves, abelian varieties, and related topics.

faculty.name: Matthew D. Foreman
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.sub_field: Ergodic Theory, Dynamical Systems, and Logic
faculty.summary: Research at the interface between dynamical systems, ergodic theory, and mathematical logic, applying set-theoretic methods including forcing, large cardinals, and descriptive set theory to problems in ergodic theory and smooth dynamics.

faculty.name: Isaac M. Goldbring
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in model theory with applications to other areas of mathematics via nonstandard analysis, including the model theory of metric structures and operator algebras.

faculty.name: Anton Gorodetski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in ergodic theory and dynamical systems, including topological, smooth, and Hamiltonian dynamics.

faculty.name: Patrick Q. Guidotti
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in fluid dynamics and free boundary problems, nonlinear partial differential equations, and optimization and data science.

faculty.name: Hamid Hezari
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in analysis and partial differential equations, with interests in geometric PDE and spectral theory.

faculty.name: Paata Ivanisvili
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in analysis and probability, contributing to both the Analysis and PDE group and the Probability group at UCI.

faculty.name: Nathan Kaplan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in number theory, coding theory, and cryptography, including work on varieties over finite fields within the Algebra and Number Theory group.

faculty.name: Katya Krupchyk
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in inverse problems and imaging, including travel times tomography, inverse scattering, wave propagation in random media, remote sensing, optical imaging, and cloaking.

faculty.name: Yi Lai
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in geometry and topology, with expertise in geometric PDE and differential geometry.

faculty.name: Song-Ying Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in function theory of several complex variables and harmonic analysis, contributing to the Analysis and PDE group.

faculty.name: John Lowengrub
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies mathematical and computational modeling to problems in materials science and biological systems, including tumor growth, tissue engineering, microstructured materials, multiphase flows, and crystal growth.

faculty.name: Zhiqin Lu
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.summary: Research in differential geometry and geometric analysis, contributing to the Geometry and Topology group.

faculty.name: Anna Ma
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning and data science, numerical analysis and scientific computing, and optimization.

faculty.name: Connor Mooney
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in analysis and partial differential equations, including nonlinear elliptic PDE and regularity theory.

faculty.name: Christopher Miles
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in applied mathematics with applications to biology and biophysics.

faculty.name: Angxiu Ni
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in applied dynamical systems, machine learning and data science, optimization, and stochastic processes.

faculty.name: Qing Nie
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational models for tissue patterning and systems biology, integrating numerical analysis and high-performance computing to study developmental and cell biology, stem cells, and cancer.

faculty.name: Manuel Reyes
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in noncommutative geometry and algebra within the Algebra and Number Theory group.

faculty.name: Knut Solna
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in applied mathematics, applied probability, and stochastic differential equations, with applications to inverse problems, imaging, wave propagation in random media, and mathematical finance.

faculty.name: Jeffrey D. Streets
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in geometry and topology, focusing on geometric partial differential equations and differential geometry.

faculty.name: Li-Sheng Tseng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in differential geometry, symplectic and contact geometry, integrable systems, and mirror symmetry, with connections to mathematical physics.

faculty.name: Roman Vershynin
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.summary: Research in high-dimensional probability, random matrix theory, and the mathematical foundations of machine learning and data science, contributing to both Applied Mathematics and Probability groups.

faculty.name: Jeff Viaclovsky
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in geometry and topology, including geometric PDE, smooth 4-manifolds, and differential geometry.

faculty.name: Jesse Wolfson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in low-dimensional topology, knot theory and its invariants, and related areas within the Geometry and Topology group.

faculty.name: Jack Xin
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in analysis and modeling of reaction-diffusion-advection fronts, stochastic partial differential equations, multiscale analysis and computation, with applications to mathematical biology, speech and hearing sciences, and mathematical finance.

faculty.name: Yifeng Yu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in fluid dynamics and free boundary problems, nonlinear partial differential equations including Hamilton-Jacobi equations, homogenization and weak KAM theory, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Martin Zeman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in set theory, particularly inner model theory and fine structure theory, within the Logic and Foundations group.

faculty.name: Xiangwen Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in geometry and topology, focusing on geometric PDE and differential geometry.

programs.program_name: Mathematical, Computational, and Systems Biology, Ph.D.

requirements.admission_model: Recommended complete-application date Dec 10; gateway PhD
