faculty.name: Jun Allard
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical models of cell mechanics, studying how cells generate and respond to forces during actin-based crawling, internal transport, and cytoskeleton dynamics using nonlinear PDEs, stochastic simulation, and Bayesian statistics. Joint appointment in Mathematics and Physics & Astronomy.

faculty.name: Pierre Baldi
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Institute for Genomics and Bioinformatics; research spans machine learning, deep learning, and their applications to bioinformatics, systems biology, genomics, and cheminformatics. Joint appointment in Biological Chemistry, Computer Science, Developmental & Cell Biology, and Statistics.

faculty.name: Anne Calof
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies neurodevelopment and stem cell biology using systems biology approaches to understand feedback regulation in developing tissues. Joint appointment in Anatomy & Neurobiology and Developmental & Cell Biology.

faculty.name: Naomi Chesler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in cardiovascular biomechanics and computational modeling of vascular function and disease. Appointed in Biomedical Engineering.

faculty.name: Olivier Cinquin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses mathematical modeling and systems biology approaches to study gene regulatory networks and developmental biology processes. Appointed in Developmental & Cell Biology.

faculty.name: Michelle Digman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops quantitative fluorescence imaging and biophotonics techniques for studying cellular and molecular dynamics. Appointed in Biomedical Engineering.

faculty.name: Fangyuan Ding
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in single-cell genomics and computational biology, developing methods for analyzing single-cell epigenomic and transcriptomic data. Appointed in Biomedical Engineering.

faculty.name: Timothy Downing
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in epigenomics and systems biology, studying epigenetic regulation in stem cell differentiation and disease using computational and genomic approaches. Appointed in Biomedical Engineering.

faculty.name: German A. Enciso Ruiz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops generic modules for biochemical reactions and investigates signal transduction pathways tied to EGF receptors, applying computational modeling to understand complex biological signaling systems. Appointed in Mathematics.

faculty.name: Charless Fowlkes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer vision and machine learning with applications to computational biology, including biological image analysis. Appointed in Computer Science.

faculty.name: Steven Frank
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical and evolutionary theory to study biological organization at multiple scales, from molecular genetics to populations. Appointed in Ecology & Evolutionary Biology.

faculty.name: Anna Grosberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computational modeling to study cardiac tissue mechanics and architecture, integrating mechanical and biological factors in heart function and disease. Appointed in Biomedical Engineering.

faculty.name: Steven Gross
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies intracellular transport and molecular motors using quantitative biophysical approaches. Joint appointment in Developmental & Cell Biology and Physics & Astronomy.

faculty.name: Wayne Hayes
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computational biology and bioinformatics, developing algorithms for biological network analysis and comparative genomics. Appointed in Computer Science.

faculty.name: Elliot Hui
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops microfluidic platforms and synthetic biology approaches to study cell-cell communication and multicellular patterning. Appointed in Biomedical Engineering.

faculty.name: Arthur Lander
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses mathematical modeling and systems biology to study pattern formation and morphogenesis in developing tissues, focusing on how feedback regulation controls growth and patterning. Joint appointment in Biomedical Engineering, Developmental & Cell Biology, and Logic & Philosophy of Science.

faculty.name: Chang Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in synthetic biology and protein engineering, developing directed evolution methods and genetically encoded biosensors. Joint appointment in Biomedical Engineering and Chemistry.

faculty.name: Beth Lopour
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Applies computational modeling, dynamical systems analysis, and signal processing to study brain activity, sleep, and epilepsy. Joint appointment in Biomedical Engineering and Mechanical & Aerospace Engineering.

faculty.name: John Lowengrub
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.summary: Applies mathematical and computational modeling to problems in materials science and biological systems, including tumor growth, tissue engineering, microstructured materials, multiphase flows, and crystal growth. Joint appointment in Mathematics, Biomedical Engineering, and Chemical Engineering.

faculty.name: Volodymyr Minin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical and computational methods for molecular evolution, phylogenetics, and infectious disease dynamics using Bayesian inference. Appointed in Statistics.

faculty.name: Eric Mjolsness
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical models and machine learning methods for computational biology, with applications to developmental biology and systems biology. Appointed in Computer Science.

faculty.name: Ali Mortazavi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computational genomics and systems biology, developing methods for analyzing transcriptomics, gene regulation, and single-cell genomics data. Appointed in Developmental & Cell Biology.

faculty.name: Qing Nie
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational models for tissue patterning and systems biology, integrating numerical analysis and high-performance computing to study developmental and cell biology, stem cells, and cancer. Joint appointment in Mathematics, Biomedical Engineering, and Developmental & Cell Biology.

faculty.name: Thorsten Ritz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies quantum effects in biological systems, including the biophysical mechanisms of magnetoreception in migratory birds. Appointed in Physics & Astronomy.

faculty.name: Babak Shahbaba
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops Bayesian statistical and machine learning methods with applications to computational biology and neuroscience. Appointed in Statistics.

faculty.name: Padhraic Smyth
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.sub_field: Machine Learning, Data Science, Computational Biology
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning, data science, and statistical modeling with applications to computational biology and environmental science. Joint appointment in Computer Science and Statistics.

faculty.name: Knut Solna
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in applied mathematics, applied probability, and stochastic differential equations, with applications to wave propagation in random media, imaging, and complex biological systems. Appointed in Mathematics.

faculty.name: Roman Vershynin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in high-dimensional probability, random matrix theory, and the mathematical foundations of machine learning and data science. Appointed in Mathematics.

faculty.name: Xiaohui Xie
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computational biology and bioinformatics, applying machine learning and deep learning methods to genomics and biomedical data analysis. Appointed in Computer Science.

faculty.name: Jack Xin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in analysis and modeling of reaction-diffusion-advection fronts, stochastic partial differential equations, and multiscale analysis, with applications to mathematical biology and data science. Appointed in Mathematics.

faculty.name: Jing Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computational biology and bioinformatics, developing methods for single-cell data analysis and integrative genomics. Appointed in Computer Science.

faculty.name: Elliot Botvinick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies mechanobiology and cell mechanics using advanced microfabrication and optical techniques to understand how physical forces influence cell behavior. Joint appointment in Biomedical Engineering, Materials Science, and Surgery.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Read
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computational modeling and stochastic simulation to study gene regulatory networks and design synthetic biological circuits. Appointed in Chemical & Biomolecular Engineering.

faculty.name: Kevin Thornton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational and statistical methods for population genetics and evolutionary biology, studying how evolutionary forces shape genetic variation. Appointed in Ecology & Evolutionary Biology.

funding.note: Guaranteed TA with full tuition & fee remission for 5 yrs

faculty.name: James McKernan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: McKernan's research concerns birational geometry, for which he has received numerous awards including the Breakthrough Prize in Mathematics and the Cole Prize in Algebra. His work focuses on the classification of algebraic varieties and the minimal model program.

faculty.name: Kiran Kedlaya
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kedlaya is an expert on arithmetic algebraic geometry and number theory, with particular focus on p-adic cohomology, p-adic Hodge theory, and computational number theory. He earned his Ph.D. from MIT and held positions at MSRI, UC Berkeley, IAS, and MIT before joining UCSD.

faculty.name: Cristian Popescu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Popescu is an expert in algebraic number theory and arithmetic geometry, specializing in Iwasawa theory, special values of L-functions, K-theory, and etale cohomology. He is a Fellow of the AMS and received the Simion Stoilow Prize of the Romanian Academy of Science.

faculty.name: Daniel Kane
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kane holds a dual appointment with Computer Science and Engineering and works on number theory and combinatorics. He and coauthors won the Godel Prize in 2026 for work on computational statistics, bridging pure mathematics and theoretical computer science.

faculty.name: Peter Ebenfelt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Ebenfelt's research spans complex variables, partial differential equations, and complex geometry. He earned his Ph.D. from the Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm and has served as department chair.

faculty.name: Bennett Chow
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chow's research focuses on geometric analysis, differential geometry, and geometric flows including Ricci flow. He earned his Ph.D. from Princeton University in 1986 and has made fundamental contributions to the study of geometric evolution equations.

faculty.name: Lei Ni
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Ni's research spans geometric analysis, Riemannian geometry, complex geometry and analysis, and partial differential equations. He is a Fellow of the AMS and a Sloan Fellow.

faculty.name: Luca Spolaor
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Spolaor specializes in geometric measure theory at the intersection of geometry and analysis/PDE. His work focuses on singular solutions to variational problems arising from physics, economics, and topology. He earned his Ph.D. from University of Zurich and held positions at Max Planck Institute and MIT.

faculty.name: Andrej Zlatos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Zlatos's research in applied analysis is motivated by real-world phenomena, focusing on partial differential equations that model reactive-diffusive processes, fluid motions, and mixing. He studies mixing in fluid dynamics and front propagation in reaction-diffusion equations.

faculty.name: Jacob Sterbenz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Sterbenz studies non-linear geometric wave equations and dispersive PDE, with a primary focus on field equations in special and general relativity. His work also involves microlocal analysis and nonlinear wave equations.

faculty.name: Todd Kemp
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kemp works in probability theory with interests in random matrix theory, free probability, and stochastic analysis. He earned his Ph.D. from UCSD and returned as faculty after positions at MIT.

faculty.name: Jason Schweinsberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Schweinsberg's research focuses on probability theory with emphasis on stochastic processes involving coalescence, branching Brownian motion, and interacting particle systems. Much of his recent work is motivated by applications in population genetics. He is a Fellow of the Institute of Mathematical Statistics.

faculty.name: Tianyi Zheng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Zheng's research lies in probability theory, with connections to geometric group theory and random walks on groups. She studies asymptotic behavior of random processes on infinite groups and related structures.

faculty.name: Dimitris Politis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Politis's research focuses on statistics including nonparametric methods, bootstrap techniques, and time series analysis. He earned his Ph.D. in Statistics from Stanford University and has made influential contributions to resampling methodology.

faculty.name: Jelena Bradic
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Bradic's research spans high-dimensional statistics, stochastic optimization, asymptotic theory, and robust statistics, with applications to functional genomics and biostatistics. She earned her Ph.D. from Princeton University and has received the Hellman Fellowship.

faculty.name: Ery Arias-Castro
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Arias-Castro's research spans statistics, machine learning, and high-dimensional inference with applications in biology, mechanical engineering, and signal processing. His work focuses on detection, clustering, and estimation in complex data settings.

faculty.name: Ronghui (Lily) Xu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Xu's research in statistics and biostatistics focuses on survival analysis, clinical trials, epidemiology, and genetic epidemiology. She develops statistical methodology for biomedical applications including cancer research and genomic studies.

faculty.name: Ioana Dumitriu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Mathematics of Information, Data, and Signals
faculty.summary: Dumitriu's research spans numerical linear algebra, scientific computing, and stochastic eigenanalysis (random matrix theory), with applications in machine learning for clustering and community detection. She earned her Ph.D. from MIT and joined UCSD in 2019 after a faculty career at University of Washington.

faculty.name: Rayan Saab
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Mathematics of Information, Data, and Signals
faculty.summary: Saab's research centers on the mathematics of signal processing, data, and information theory, including compressed sensing, quantization, and applied harmonic analysis. He joined UCSD in 2013 after a Banting Postdoctoral Fellowship at Duke University.

faculty.name: Melvin Leok
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leok's research in computational geometric mechanics develops structure-preserving numerical methods that provide stable long-time solutions to differential equations for modeling and controlling modern engineering systems, synthesizing geometry and numerical analysis.

faculty.name: Michael Holst
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Holst's research spans numerical differential equations, mathematical modeling, mathematical physics, and mathematical biology. He develops computational methods for PDEs in applications ranging from biophysics to general relativity. He is a SIAM Fellow and NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Jiawang Nie
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Nie's research is in numerical analysis and scientific computing, emphasizing polynomial and semidefinite optimization, moment and tensor computation, and convex real algebraic geometry. He earned his Ph.D. from UC Berkeley and received the Feng Kang Prize and an NSF CAREER Award.

faculty.name: Bo Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Li's research spans mathematical modeling and applied analysis, mathematical biology, biological physics, and continuum mechanics. His work develops mathematical frameworks for modeling complex physical and biological systems using PDE and dynamical systems.

faculty.name: Frederick Manners
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Manners's research centers on arithmetic pseudorandomness -- investigating whether mathematical structures appear random or harbor hidden patterns -- through an in-depth study of Gowers norms and their inverse theory, with applications to combinatorics, number theory, and ergodic theory.

faculty.name: Andrew Suk
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Suk's main research area is geometric combinatorics, spanning extremal combinatorics, Ramsey theory, discrete geometry, and combinatorial number theory. He earned his Ph.D. from NYU Courant and received an NSF CAREER Award and Sloan Research Fellowship.

faculty.name: Jacques Verstraete
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Verstraete's research in combinatorics and graph theory focuses on extremal problems, Turan-type questions, and Ramsey theory. He earned his Ph.D. from Cambridge University and holds an Alfred P. Sloan Research Fellowship.

faculty.name: Brendon Rhoades
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Rhoades's research in algebraic combinatorics uses representation-theoretic tools connected to Kazhdan-Lusztig theory and cluster algebras to derive enumerative results related to the cyclic sieving phenomenon and Coxeter-Catalan combinatorics.

faculty.name: Steven Sam
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Sam's research sits at the intersection of combinatorics, representation theory, and algebraic geometry, centered on representation stability and invariant theory. His work on twisted commutative algebras with Snowden solved the Lannes-Schwartz artinian conjecture.

faculty.name: Alireza Salehi Golsefidy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Salehi Golsefidy's research focuses on discrete subgroups of Lie groups, spanning algebra, number theory, combinatorics, arithmetic lattices, and homogeneous dynamical systems. He earned his Ph.D. at Yale under Fields Medalist Gregory Margulis and received a Sloan Research Fellowship.

faculty.name: Kristin DeVleming
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: DeVleming's research in algebraic geometry focuses on moduli of varieties, K-stability, birational geometry, and singularities. She earned her Ph.D. from University of Washington and held positions at MSRI/SLMath and UMass Amherst before joining UCSD in 2024.

faculty.name: Morris Ang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Ang's research focuses on random conformal geometry, including Schramm-Loewner evolution, Liouville quantum gravity, random planar maps, and conformal field theories. He earned his Ph.D. from MIT in 2022 and was a Junior Fellow at Columbia University before joining UCSD in 2024.

funding.note: Meeting deadline required for departmental/campus financial support consideration

faculty.name: Adebisi Agboola
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in number theory.

faculty.name: Amalendu Krishna
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in algebraic and arithmetic geometry, with a focus on algebraic K-theory.

faculty.name: Bjorn Birnir
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in nonlinear partial differential equations. Serves as Department Chair.

faculty.name: Carlos Garcia-Cervera
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of applied mathematics, materials science, and physics, focusing on mathematical aspects of materials science, calculus of variations, micromagnetics and the mathematical theory of liquid crystals, mathematical physics and quantum chemistry, and scientific computing.

faculty.name: Christopher Ograin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests span applied mathematics and mathematics education. Serves as Undergraduate Vice Chair.

faculty.name: David Morrison
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor of Mathematics and Physics. Research at the intersection of algebraic geometry and string theory, with work on special holonomy manifolds, quantum fields and strings, and connections between geometry and high-energy physics. Affiliated with KITP and the Simons Collaboration on Special Holonomy.

faculty.name: Davit Harutyunyan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in applied analysis with focus on materials science, including sharp Korn inequalities and geometric rigidity of thin structures, shell buckling and elasticity theory, quasiconvexity and effective elasticity tensors of composites, and applications to ferromagnetic nanowires and magnetic thin films. Funded by NSF DMS grants.

faculty.name: Denis Labutin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in partial differential equations.

faculty.name: Francesc Castella
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in number theory and arithmetic geometry, centered on p-adic L-functions, Euler systems, and Iwasawa theory. Constructs anticyclotomic Euler systems and proves Iwasawa main conjectures for GL(2), including at Eisenstein primes. Works on Heegner points, Birch-Swinnerton-Dyer formula, and Tamagawa number conjectures. Supported by NSF DMS-2401321 and 2024-2025 AMS Centennial Research Fellowship.

faculty.name: Guofang Wei
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in differential geometry and geometric analysis, focusing on global Riemannian geometry and the interaction of curvature with underlying geometry and topology. Topics include fundamental groups, comparison geometry, manifolds with integral curvature bounds, spaces with weak curvature bounds, and eigenvalue problems for the Laplacian. Currently advises multiple PhD students.

faculty.name: Gustavo Ponce
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of mathematical physics, harmonic analysis, and nonlinear partial differential equations, studying the qualitative behavior of solutions arising from physical models using tools from harmonic analysis.

faculty.name: Hanming Zhou
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on mathematical analysis of inverse problems, bridging PDEs, microlocal analysis, differential geometry, and mathematical physics. Topics include X-ray and Radon transforms, travel time tomography in stationary spacetimes, lens rigidity, transport equations, and inverse source problems. Applications in medical imaging, geophysics, and astronomy. Open to supervising graduate students; research assistantships available. Funded by NSF DMS-2408369.

faculty.name: Hector D. Ceniceros
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in numerical analysis, scientific machine learning, soft materials and complex fluids, and free boundary problems. Fellow of the American Mathematical Society (Class of 2019). Partner of the Complex Fluids Design Consortium. PhD from Courant Institute (NYU), postdoc at Caltech Applied Mathematics.

faculty.name: Jon McCammond
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in geometric group theory, with work on associahedra and noncrossing partitions, studying the geometry of subdivided spaces and reflection groups. Connects algebra, geometry, and combinatorics within geometric group theory.

faculty.name: Katy Craig
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of nonlinear PDEs, optimal transport theory, calculus of variations, and numerical analysis. Develops blob methods for diffusion, aggregation, and mean-field control. Works on Wasserstein gradient flows and primal-dual computation methods. Applies optimal transport to high-energy physics collider data and develops wavelet-based Wasserstein distances. Holds NSF CAREER award. Maintains the Optimal Transport Wiki (otwiki.xyz) and organizes graduate summer schools. Welco

faculty.name: Maria Isabel Bueno Cachadina
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in numerical linear algebra, matrix analysis, and orthogonal polynomials.

faculty.name: Matt Jacobs
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans calculus of variations, optimization, numerical methods, PDEs, optimal transport, free boundary problems, and adversarial learning. Combines theoretical mathematics with computational methods and applied domains. PhD from University of Michigan (2017).

faculty.name: Matthias Wink
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in Riemannian geometry and geometric analysis, centered on the Bochner technique, Einstein metrics, Ricci solitons, and Ricci flow. Work on vanishing theorems for Hodge numbers, holonomy restrictions, and construction of expanding Ricci solitons and Einstein metrics on spheres. Publications in Inventiones mathematicae, Journal fur die reine und angewandte Mathematik, and Advances in Mathematics. DPhil from Oxford under Andrew Dancer.

faculty.name: Mihai Putinar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in complex analysis and operator theory, with work on moment problems, matrix positivity, polynomial optimization, Christoffel-Darboux kernel and applications, distance geometry, superresolution, and Laplacian growth. Involved with the POEMA network on polynomial optimization.

faculty.name: Paul Atzberger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of mathematical analysis, machine learning, numerical analysis, and scientific computation. Develops Geometric Neural Operators (GNPs) for deep learning on manifolds, GMLS-Nets for learning differential operators from scattered data, and GD-VAEs for nonlinear dynamics with dimension reduction. Also works on fluctuating hydrodynamics, curved fluid interfaces, stochastic fluid-structure interactions, and non-equilibrium statistical mechanics. Held NSF CAREER award. Sup

faculty.name: Peter Garfield
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests in differential geometry and mathematics education.

faculty.name: Rugang Ye
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in differential geometry and geometric analysis, including the Ricci flow and geometrization of lower dimensional manifolds, and mathematical physics.

faculty.name: Ruimeng Hu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of machine learning, financial mathematics, and game theory. Works on applying modern ML techniques to quantitative finance, stochastic control and games, and mean field approximations. Jointly appointed in Mathematics and Statistics & Applied Probability. Co-organized SIAG/FME Code Quest 2023 on DeFi and RoboAdvising and the ACM International Conference on AI in Finance.

faculty.name: Stephen Bigelow
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in topology with a focus on diagrammatic algebra, studying mathematical structures that combine topological concepts with algebraic representations, including braid groups, knot theory, and the Burau representation of the braid group.

faculty.name: Sui Tang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on mathematical foundations of data science, focusing on learning dynamics from complex physical systems. Develops methods with provable performance for inference on dynamical data, including learning interaction kernels in particle systems using Gaussian processes and neural ODEs. Investigates theoretical properties of graph neural networks and transformers. Holds NSF CAREER Award (2340631). Welcomes inquiries from students interested in exploring mathematics of AI. Funded by NSF, Simo

faculty.name: Xianzhe Dai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in differential geometry and geometric analysis, studying geometry and analysis on manifolds. Organizes international conferences on metric and differential geometry.

faculty.name: Xiaolei Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in algebraic geometry, specializing in derived categories and Bridgeland stability conditions on Fano threefolds, K3 surfaces, Enriques surfaces, Gushel-Mukai varieties, and cubic fourfolds. Studies moduli spaces of coherent sheaves, hyperkähler and holomorphic symplectic varieties including O'Grady 10 varieties, and Torelli theorems. Part of NSF FRG on Derived Categories, Moduli Spaces, and Classical Algebraic Geometry (DMS-2052665). PhD from University of Michigan (2015) under Herb Cl

faculty.name: Xu Yang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in applied and computational mathematics spanning semiclassical theory, seismic tomography, and deep learning. Develops frozen Gaussian approximation (FGA) for high-frequency wave propagation and seismic imaging, with an open-source API (GeoSeg) for deep learning-based seismic interface detection. Works on superconvergent finite element methods for interface problems, including topological edge modes in photonic graphene. Studies spin-magnetization coupling in ferromagnetic materials vi

faculty.name: Zheng Liu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in number theory on p-adic properties of automorphic forms and special L-values, focusing on p-adic L-functions, modular forms, and Iwasawa theory. Constructs p-adic L-functions for groups like GSp(4)xGL(2) and studies the Iwasawa Greenberg main conjecture for non-ordinary modular forms. Works on Siegel modular forms, non-cuspidal Hida theory, zeta integrals for unitary groups, and derived p-adic heights. Publications in Trans. AMS, Math. Ann., and J. Inst. Math. Jussieu.

faculty.name: Zhenghan Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of mathematics, quantum physics, and computation, focusing on quantum topology/algebra and topological quantum computing. Affiliated with Microsoft Station Q and Perimeter Institute. Builds rigorous mathematical foundations for fault-tolerant quantum computation using topological phases of matter. Teaches course on Mathematical Foundations of Topological Quantum Computing.

faculty.name: Zijian Yao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Faculty member in the Department of Mathematics.

funding.note: Fully funded PhD

faculty.name: Frank Calegari
faculty.title: Professor (Chair)
faculty.summary: Works on algebraic number theory, particularly the Langlands program and reciprocity linking Galois representations to automorphic forms. Also studies the cohomology of arithmetic groups, especially torsion classes.

faculty.name: László Babai
faculty.title: George and Elizabeth Yovovich Professor
faculty.summary: Works in theoretical computer science and discrete mathematics, with emphasis on computational complexity theory, algorithms, combinatorics, and finite groups. Known for introducing Las Vegas algorithms, interactive proofs, and holographic proofs, as well as the graph isomorphism breakthrough.

faculty.name: Guillaume Bal
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on inverse problems theory with applications in geophysical and medical imaging, particularly inverse transport theory and coupled-physics (hybrid) imaging modalities. Also studies PDE models with random coefficients and propagation of stochasticity, including wave propagation in heterogeneous environments and topological insulators.

faculty.name: Alexander Beilinson
faculty.title: David and Mary Winton Green University Professor
faculty.summary: Works in arithmetic algebraic geometry and the geometric Langlands program. Shaw Prize laureate (2020) for his foundational contributions to representation theory, algebraic geometry, and mathematical physics.

faculty.name: Danny Calegari
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests include geometry, topology, and dynamics in low dimensions; foliations and laminations; and extremal problems in topology and geometric group theory. PhD from Berkeley (2000).

faculty.name: Jiajie Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on singularity formation in nonlinear PDEs, particularly mathematical fluid mechanics. Studies self-similar singularities in compressible Euler equations and finite-time blowup of 3D incompressible Euler equations, combining ODE techniques, weighted energy estimates, and numerical PDE methods for rigorous analysis. PhD from Caltech; previously Courant Instructor at NYU.

faculty.name: Kevin Corlette
faculty.title: George and Elizabeth Yovovich Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on differential and algebraic geometry, with particular interest in Kähler geometry, locally symmetric spaces, and systems of PDEs with geometric meaning such as the harmonic map and Yang-Mills equations. Director of the Institute for Mathematical and Statistical Innovation.

faculty.name: Marianna Csörnyei
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in geometric measure theory, real analysis, and geometric nonlinear functional analysis. Recently focused on problems related to Lipschitz differentiability, tangential properties, structure of null sets, and the Kakeya property. Noether Lecture speaker (2021).

faculty.name: Yu Deng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on probabilistic PDE theory, wave turbulence and kinetic theory, statistical physics, and quantum field theory. Research includes long-time derivation of the Boltzmann equation from hard sphere dynamics, invariant Gibbs measures for nonlinear wave and Schrödinger equations, and instability of Couette flow. PhD from Princeton (2015) under A. Ionescu.

faculty.name: Vladimir Drinfeld
faculty.title: Harry Pratt Judson Distinguished Service Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on the geometric Langlands program, a part of geometric representation theory. Also works on character sheaf theory for unipotent groups, building on Lusztig's work to study irreducible characters across all finite-field extensions simultaneously using perverse sheaves. Fields Medalist (1990).

faculty.name: Matthew Emerton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in number theory and arithmetic geometry, with particular interest in automorphic forms and their relationship to Diophantine equations and the Galois theory of number fields through the Langlands Program. PhD from Harvard.

faculty.name: Alex Eskin
faculty.title: Arthur Holly Compton Distinguished Service Professor
faculty.summary: Works in the theory of dynamical systems with applications to geometry and number theory. Recipient of the Breakthrough Prize in Mathematics (2019). PhD from Princeton under Peter Sarnak.

faculty.name: Benson Farb
faculty.title: Ann Gillian Sheldon Professor
faculty.summary: Has published in geometric group theory, low-dimensional topology, dynamical systems, differential geometry, Teichmüller theory, cohomology of arithmetic groups, representation theory, algebraic geometry, and 4-manifold theory, with emphasis on connections between these areas. PhD from Princeton under W.P. Thurston.

faculty.name: Robert Fefferman
faculty.title: Max Mason Distinguished Service Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on harmonic analysis and elliptic partial differential equations. AMS Fellow (2022). PhD from Princeton.

faculty.name: Simion Filip
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Dynamical Systems / Complex and Algebraic Geometry
faculty.summary: Interested in the interactions of dynamical systems with other parts of mathematics, particularly complex and algebraic geometry. EMS Prize winner (2020). PhD from University of Chicago under Alex Eskin.

faculty.name: Victor Ginzburg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in geometric representation theory and noncommutative geometry. Applies algebraic geometry methods to study representations of algebras, including classification of Hecke algebra representations, D-modules on reductive groups, and integrable representations of quantum groups via quiver varieties. Co-author of Chriss-Ginzburg on Representation Theory and Complex Geometry. Currently advises 7 PhD students.

faculty.name: Ewain Gwynne
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in probability theory with focus on random geometric objects from statistical mechanics, including Schramm-Loewner evolution, Liouville quantum gravity, random planar maps, random permutations, and random walk in random environment. PhD from MIT (2018) advised by Scott Sheffield.

faculty.name: Denis Hirschfeldt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in computability theory with focus on computable model theory, computable algebra, algorithmic randomness, and the computability-theoretic and reverse mathematical analysis of combinatorial principles. Co-author of Algorithmic Randomness and Complexity with Downey.

faculty.name: Kazuya Kato
faculty.title: Samuel K. Allison Distinguished Service Professor
faculty.summary: A leading figure in number theory and arithmetic geometry, known for foundational contributions to K-theory, Iwasawa theory, p-adic Hodge theory, and class field theory. Profile page was sparse; research interests inferred from his well-known body of work.

faculty.name: Carlos Kenig
faculty.title: Louis Block Distinguished Service Professor
faculty.summary: Research is in analysis and partial differential equations. A world-leading figure in elliptic and dispersive PDE, harmonic analysis, and nonlinear evolution equations.

faculty.name: Gregory F. Lawler
faculty.title: George Wells Beadle Distinguished Service Professor
faculty.summary: Works on random walks and continuous analogues (Brownian motion and diffusions), focusing on processes from statistical physics including self-avoiding random walk, loop-erased random walk, and percolation. Studies conformally invariant quantities such as Schramm-Loewner evolution, Gaussian free field, and loop measures in two dimensions. Also explores applications of probability to complex analysis.

faculty.name: Roger Lee
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in financial mathematics focusing on volatility derivatives (variance swaps, corridor variance swaps), implied volatility asymptotics, robust replication, and more recently automated market makers and DeFi microstructure. Faculty Director of the Financial Mathematics Program. PhD from University of Chicago.

faculty.name: Maryanthe Malliaris
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests are in model theory, particularly the classification of theories. Quantrell Award recipient (2019) for undergraduate teaching.

faculty.name: Akhil Mathew
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Primary research in algebraic topology (especially stable homotopy theory) and algebraic K-theory, with growing interest in p-adic arithmetic geometry and its interactions with these subjects. Works on topological Hochschild homology, descent theory, and K-theory of perfectoid rings. PhD from Harvard under Jacob Lurie.

faculty.name: J. Peter May
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans a variety of topics in and around algebraic topology, with recent emphasis on equivariant stable homotopy theory and equivariant infinite loop space theory. Notes that equivariant versions of chromatic homotopy theory and homotopy groups of spheres are virtually unexplored territory. PhD from Princeton (1964).

faculty.name: André Neves
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the interplay between topology, geometry, and analysis, using variational methods to study how curvature and topology of ambient spaces influence the existence of stable and unstable critical points of geometric functionals. Elected to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, Simons Investigator.

faculty.name: Bảo Châu Ngô
faculty.title: Francis and Rose Yuen Distinguished Service Professor
faculty.sub_field: Automorphic Forms / Langlands Program / Algebraic Geometry
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of automorphic forms, representation theory, and algebraic geometry, with focus on the Langlands program. Fields Medalist (2010) for his proof of the Fundamental Lemma. Current work includes geometrization of the trace formula, Hitchin fibration for higher-dimensional varieties, and Kloosterman sheaves for reductive groups.

faculty.name: Alexander Razborov
faculty.title: Andrew McLeish Distinguished Service Professor
faculty.sub_field: Continuous Combinatorics / Proof Complexity / Theoretical CS
faculty.summary: Works in logic, theoretical computer science, and combinatorics. Currently active in two areas: continuous combinatorics (flag algebras, graph limits, connecting to algebra, analysis, measure theory, logic, and probability) and propositional proof complexity (understanding which true statements have efficient proofs and the principles behind practical SAT solvers). PhD from Steklov Mathematical Institute.

faculty.name: Daniil Rudenko
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on connections between multiple polylogarithms and algebraic K-theory, including the Goncharov program, scissors congruence of hyperbolic polytopes, mixed Tate motives, and volumes of non-Euclidean polytopes. Proved the Strong Suslin Reciprocity Law (Compositio Mathematica, 2021). Also works on rational elliptic surfaces from tetrahedra and cluster algebras.

faculty.name: Tomer Schlank
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Homotopy Theory / Higher Algebra / Chromatic Homotopy
faculty.summary: Works in homotopy theory, algebraic geometry, and number theory. Co-disproved the telescope conjecture for all heights (one of Ravenel's conjectures), proved the Chromatic Nullstellensatz resolving the Ausoni-Rognes redshift conjecture, and calculated homotopy groups of the rationalized K(n)-local sphere. Clay Research Award (2026). Developed étale homotopy obstructions to rational points.

faculty.name: Luis Silvestre
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on regularity theory and qualitative behavior of PDEs, particularly fractional and nonlocal operators, kinetic equations (Boltzmann and Landau equations), and parabolic equations. Proved that the Landau equation does not blow up. Serves on editorial boards of JAMS, Journal of Functional Analysis, and Annals of PDE.

faculty.name: Panagiotis Souganidis
faculty.title: Charles H. Swift Distinguished Service Professor
faculty.summary: Works in partial differential equations, investigating qualitative properties of solutions to elliptic/parabolic and hyperbolic PDEs (conservation laws, Hamilton-Jacobi equations). Research includes stochastic viscosity solutions for fully nonlinear stochastic PDE, homogenization in random environments, and biological models for motor effects.

faculty.name: Shmuel Weinberger
faculty.title: Andrew MacLeish Distinguished Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Research concerned with understanding things geometrically. Five main directions: topology of manifolds (especially high-dimensional), global analysis (L2 cohomology, index theory on noncompact manifolds, Novikov conjecture), singularities (orbifolds and stratified spaces), applications of logic and CS to variational problems and moduli spaces, and quantitative topology. Also works on applications of algebraic topology to data science, robotics, and economics.

faculty.name: Amie Wilkinson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in smooth dynamical systems, focusing on the interplay between dynamics and other structures in pure mathematics — geometric, statistical, topological, and algebraic. Studies mechanisms for chaotic behavior (partial hyperbolicity, stable mixing), how Riemannian geometry produces chaotic geodesic distribution, and rigidity vs. flexibility of smooth dynamical systems. Also exploring connections between smooth dynamics and particle accelerator design physics. Has funding for undergraduate thr

funding.note: TA stipend, tuition waiver, health insurance; ~4 years support

faculty.name: Henry Adams
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in applied and computational topology, metric geometry, and their connections to machine learning and sensor networks, developing topological tools for data analysis.

faculty.name: Krishnaswami Alladi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans analytic and probabilistic number theory, particularly sieve methods and Diophantine approximations, alongside combinatorial topics like integer partitions and q-series identities.

faculty.name: Dana Bartosova
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of logic and dynamics, applying Ramsey-theoretic methods to problems in topological dynamics and combinatorial number theory.

faculty.name: Alexander Berkovich
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research lies at the intersection of additive number theory (partitions, q-hypergeometric series) and mathematical physics (quantum exactly integrable models, conformal field theory, special functions).

faculty.name: Miklós Bóna
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on enumerative and analytic combinatorics, particularly permutation patterns and combinatorial structures, with recent contributions to Boolean-Narayana numbers.

faculty.name: Jeremy Booher
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on algebraic and computational number theory with an emphasis on Galois representations and curves in characteristic p within arithmetic geometry.

faculty.name: Peter Bubenik
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical frameworks for topological data analysis, integrating topology, statistics, and machine learning to quantify geometric structure in high-dimensional datasets.

faculty.name: Luca Fabrizio Di Cerbo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in differential and algebraic geometry as well as geometric topology, bridging geometric structures and topological properties of manifolds.

faculty.name: Alexander Dranishnikov
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans geometric topology including topological complexity and Lusternik-Schnirelmann category, cohomological dimension theory, asymptotic dimension of groups, and positive scalar curvature.

faculty.name: Eric Evert
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on free (matrix) convexity and extreme point theory in noncommutative settings, along with tensor decompositions and low-rank approximation problems.

faculty.name: Frank Garvan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in number theory and combinatorics focusing on q-series and integer partitions; co-discovered the crank of a partition with George Andrews, solving a problem posed by Freeman Dyson related to Ramanujan congruences.

faculty.name: David Groisser
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on differential geometry and global analysis with a focus on gauge theory and geometry-driven approaches to image analysis and statistical applications.

faculty.name: Yeor Hafuta
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research sits at the intersection of probability theory and dynamical systems, specifically focusing on ergodic theory and related probabilistic phenomena.

faculty.name: William Hager
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans optimization (gradient methods, global optimization, regularization), optimal control, numerical analysis, and applied mathematics including imaging and electronic structure computations.

faculty.name: Zachary Hamaker
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in algebraic combinatorics, applying algebraic methods to combinatorial structures.

faculty.name: Nan Jiang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops numerical methods and models for computational fluid dynamics including turbulence simulation, nonlocal models for anomalous diffusion, and ensemble methods for uncertainty quantification.

faculty.name: Michael Jury
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on noncommutative function theory and operator theory, with additional work in complex analysis, random matrix theory, and free probability.

faculty.name: Kevin Keating
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research lies at the intersection of number theory and arithmetic algebraic geometry, with additional interests in combinatorial mathematics.

faculty.name: Kevin Knudson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of computational topology and discrete Morse theory, also studying homology of linear groups, with additional interest in undergraduate mathematics education.

faculty.name: Arnaud Marsiglietti
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research sits at the intersection of high-dimensional probability, convex geometry, and information theory, exploring probabilistic and geometric properties in high-dimensional spaces.

faculty.name: Maia Martcheva
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on mathematical modeling of infectious diseases including avian influenza, malaria, spatial epidemics, multi-strain interactions, and HIV/opioid epidemics.

faculty.name: Scott McCullough
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on operator theory and functional analysis, including dilation theory, noncommutative analysis, free spectrahedra, and linear matrix inequalities.

faculty.name: Calistus Ngonghala
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research models infectious diseases at the intersection of ecology, poverty, and human behavior, integrating disease epidemiology with socio-economic factors and nonlinear dynamical systems.

faculty.name: Youngmin Park
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies dynamical systems theory to model biological processes including neural network synchronization, intracellular transport, and the system-level dynamics of sepsis.

faculty.name: Sara Pollock
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops efficient numerical solvers for nonlinear and eigenvalue problems using finite element methods, with additional work in optimal control for PDEs and goal-oriented adaptivity.

faculty.name: Paul Robinson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on operator algebras and representations of canonical commutation/anticommutation relations, alongside geometric quantization, symplectic geometry, and projective geometry.

faculty.name: Libin Rong
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies mathematical and computational modeling to biological systems, particularly infectious diseases and viral dynamics, with affiliations at the Emerging Pathogens Institute.

faculty.name: Sergei Shabanov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans quantum field theory and Yang-Mills theories, wave scattering and bound states in the continuum, and fluid dynamics applied to laser-induced plasma phenomena.

faculty.name: Peter Sin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on group theory, representation theory, and discrete mathematics, working at the intersection of algebra and discrete structures.

faculty.name: Tracy Stepien
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Applies mathematical modeling, dynamical systems, and numerical methods to biomedical problems including cell migration, embryonic development, and cancer growth and treatment.

faculty.name: Robin Tucker-Drob
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research sits at the intersection of mathematical logic, set theory, ergodic theory, and group theory, exploring connections between foundational questions and dynamical systems.

faculty.name: Alexandre Turull
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in finite group theory and representation theory, with deep contributions to Clifford theory, the Brauer-Clifford group, Schur indices of irreducible characters, and the Alperin weight and McKay conjectures for p-solvable groups.

faculty.name: Vincent Vatter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies structure and growth in discrete systems, particularly how local constraints force global behavior in permutations and the growth rates of permutation classes.

faculty.name: Andrew Vince
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans discrete mathematics and geometry, with focus on combinatorial structures, graph-theoretic problems, fractal geometry, and combinatorial algorithms.

faculty.name: Hubert Wagner
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical foundations, algorithms, and software for topological computations, with applications to astrophysics, biomedicine, and machine learning including trojan detection in neural networks.

faculty.name: Chunmei Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans data science, deep learning, and numerical methods for partial differential equations, with specialization in weak Galerkin finite element methods, inverse problems, and high-performance computing.

faculty.name: Cheng Yu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research broadly addresses partial differential equations.

faculty.name: Jindrich Zapletal
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on foundational questions in set theory and mathematical logic.

faculty.name: Lei Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on nonlinear partial differential equations and geometric analysis.

funding.note: Teaching/research assistantships typically available

faculty.name: Valery Alexeev
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Alexeev constructs and studies compact moduli spaces (such as KSBA compactifications) for classes of algebraic varieties including K3 surfaces, Enriques surfaces, Calabi-Yau varieties, and abelian varieties, with connections to the Minimal Model Program and derived categories.

faculty.name: Jason Cantarella
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Cantarella works in geometric knot theory, studying the shapes of random curves and polygons, ropelength criticality of knots, and the geometry and topology of spaces of curves. His research investigates the distribution of knot types in random space curves and the symplectic geometry of polygon spaces.

faculty.name: Pete L. Clark
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Clark's research bridges algebraic and arithmetic structures, exploring deep connections between commutative algebra, number theory, and arithmetic geometry.

faculty.name: Joseph H.G. Fu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Fu is a leading authority in integral geometry, known for resolving the problem of hermitian integral geometry and for developing algebraic integral geometry with Alesker and Bernig. His work on valuation algebras and kinematic formulas connects integral geometry to algebra and has applications in tomography and curvature measures.

faculty.name: David Gay
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gay is known for introducing trisections, a systematic decomposition of 4-dimensional spaces into three pieces, resolving long-standing obstacles in 4-manifold topology. His work focuses on smooth low-dimensional topology in dimensions 3, 4, and 5, including exotic smooth structures and diffeomorphism groups of 4-manifolds.

faculty.name: William Graham
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Graham investigates group actions on algebraic varieties, developing equivariant theories like Chow groups and K-theory with tools such as localization and Riemann-Roch, while also exploring specific Lie-theoretic varieties including flag varieties, Schubert varieties, and Springer fibers.

faculty.name: Sa'ar Hersonsky
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Hersonsky's work lies at the intersection of geometry, topology, and analysis, focusing on conformal geometry, geometric structures in low-dimensional topology, and the geometry of negatively curved spaces.

faculty.name: Weiwei Hu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Hu's research centers on optimal control and optimization of systems governed by partial differential equations, particularly fluid flows and transport processes, blending analytical methods with computational and machine learning approaches for applications in mixing, convection-cooling, and renewable energy systems.

faculty.name: Ming-Jun Lai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lai develops multivariate spline methods (piecewise polynomial functions on triangulations) for PDE solution, scattered data fitting, image processing, and tooth surface modeling, alongside work on sparse solution recovery for underdetermined linear systems and graph clustering.

faculty.name: Dino Lorenzini
faculty.title: Distinguished Research Professor
faculty.summary: Lorenzini works in number theory and arithmetic geometry, with connections to algebraic geometry. He serves as Faculty Director of the RTG in Algebra, Algebraic Geometry, and Number Theory at UGA.

faculty.name: Neil Lyall
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lyall's research explores maximal averaging operators and finite point configurations at the intersection of harmonic analysis, number theory, and additive combinatorics, supported by a Simons Foundation grant (2023-2028).

faculty.name: Akos Magyar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Magyar's work intersects analytic methods with number-theoretic and combinatorial problems in the areas of harmonic analysis and additive combinatorics.

faculty.name: Gordana Matic
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Matic works in topology and geometry, with research interests in contact topology as part of UGA's geometry research group.

faculty.name: Daniel K. Nakano
faculty.title: Distinguished Research Professor
faculty.summary: Nakano's work sits at the intersection of representation theory, geometry, and homological algebra, investigating the hidden geometry within tensor triangulated categories to gain new insights into algebraic objects and their representations, with connections to combinatorics, topology, number theory, chemistry, and physics.

faculty.name: Paul Pollack
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pollack's work centers on elementary and analytic number theory, with a particular focus on statistical questions about algebraic objects. Named a 2025 AMS Fellow, he also serves as the department's Director of Graduate Studies.

faculty.name: Jingzhi Tie
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Tie's work centers on the Heisenberg group, employing Laguerre calculus to study sub-Laplacian operators, fundamental solutions, and heat kernels, with applications extending to sub-Riemannian geometry, CR manifolds, and financial mathematics (optimal trading rules under mean-reversion models).

faculty.name: Michael Usher
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Usher studies symplectic and contact geometry, making significant contributions to symplectic embedding problems in four dimensions (including infinite staircases for ellipsoids into polydisks), deformed Hamiltonian Floer theory, and knotted symplectic embeddings. He developed the concept of boundary depth for Hamiltonian diffeomorphisms, earning a Creative Research Medal (2014).

faculty.name: Guowei Wei
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Wei pioneers topological deep learning and persistent spectral theory, integrating algebraic, differential, and geometric topology with AI for molecular biosciences including drug discovery, virus evolution prediction, and protein engineering. His group was top performer in the D3R Grand Challenges (global drug design competition) and accurately predicted key SARS-CoV-2 mutational sites months before variant emergence.

faculty.name: Qing Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Zhang investigates stochastic systems and optimal control with applications to finance, focusing on two-time-scale Markovian systems, nonlinear filtering, and related control problems. He has published five monographs and over 200 research papers.

faculty.name: Akram Alishahi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Alishahi studies low-dimensional topology and knot theory, focusing on two powerful knot invariants: Heegaard Floer homology and Khovanov homology. Her work is supported by an NSF CAREER grant.

faculty.name: Leonard Chastkofsky
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Chastkofsky's research focuses on algebra, and he teaches a range of courses including abstract algebra at the undergraduate and graduate level.

faculty.name: Caner Kazanci
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Kazanci models biological and ecological systems, studying system-wide properties such as control, evolution, and network analysis. He created EcoNet, an online ecological modeling and simulation software, and develops particle tracking methods for analyzing ecological networks and stochastic individual-based models.

faculty.name: Lin Mu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Mu develops stable, efficient numerical methods (particularly weak Galerkin finite element techniques) for challenging PDEs arising in fusion plasma physics, fluid dynamics in porous media, and electromagnetic wave propagation. His work bridges rigorous error analysis with high-performance computing for DOE and NSF-funded projects including fusion energy simulation.

faculty.name: Giorgis Petridis
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Petridis works primarily in arithmetic combinatorics, the 21st-century descendant of combinatorial number theory, alongside combinatorial geometry and discrete mathematics. He won a 2024 Creative Research Medal for work with Brandon Hanson on the Paley graph conjecture, and currently co-advises four PhD students.

faculty.name: Laura Rider
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Rider studies geometric representation theory, investigating categories of sheaves on Lie-theoretic spaces such as the nilpotent cone, affine Grassmannian, and affine flag variety. Her work touches on parity sheaves, the Springer correspondence, modular representation theory, and categorification.

faculty.name: Shuzhou Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Wang's work centers on quantum groups and noncommutative geometry, bridging operator algebras with mathematical physics at the intersection of analysis and algebraic structures.

faculty.name: Nate Harman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Harman works in algebra with focus on combinatorial representation theory, modular representations, tensor categories, and representation stability. His research also spans Tannakian formalism, model-theoretic techniques in algebra, and lattices in semisimple Lie groups with superrigidity.

faculty.name: Peter Lambert-Cole
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Lambert-Cole's research focuses on topology. He earned his PhD from Louisiana State University (2014) and an AB from Princeton University (2009).

faculty.name: Siddharth Mathur
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Mathur investigates foundational questions in algebraic geometry — particularly how vector bundles, sheaves, and cohomological invariants behave on algebraic stacks and varieties — while applying these tools to arithmetic geometry problems such as hyperbolicity, moduli spaces, and the Lang-Vojta conjecture.

faculty.name: João Pereira
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Pereira develops scalable algorithms and studies the statistical properties of high-dimensional inverse problems, spanning implicit tensor decomposition methods, physics-informed neural network architectures for differential equations, and the sample complexity of multireference alignment relevant to cryo-electron microscopy.

faculty.name: Ruiwen Shu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Shu studies interaction energies and their minimizers, investigating both theoretical properties (existence, uniqueness, symmetry) and connections to dynamical models like aggregation-diffusion equations. His work also spans numerical analysis of kinetic equations, including asymptotic-preserving and implicit-explicit schemes.

faculty.name: Tomasz Szarek
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Szarek's work centers on sharp heat kernel estimates for Jacobi and spherical expansions, dimension-free estimates for discrete maximal functions, and ergodic theorems on nilpotent groups. He also studies polynomial sequences in discrete nilpotent structures and oscillation inequalities.

faculty.name: Jiuya Wang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Wang's research bridges algebraic and analytic number theory. She earned her PhD at University of Wisconsin-Madison under Melanie Matchett Wood and previously held a Phillip Griffiths Research Assistantship at Duke University.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Brock
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies hyperbolic 3-manifolds and low-dimensional topology; co-proved the Ending Lamination Conjecture; applies geometric methods to complex data analysis.

faculty.name: Ronald Coifman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in nonlinear Fourier analysis and its applications to signal processing, data science, and medical imaging.

faculty.name: Igor Frenkel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops vertex operator algebras and infinite-dimensional representation theory with connections to the Monster group and mathematical physics.

faculty.name: Alexander Goncharov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies arithmetic algebraic geometry, motives, polylogarithms, and cluster varieties connecting number theory with representation theory.

faculty.name: Sebastian Hurtado-Salazar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates smooth group actions on manifolds, rigidity phenomena, and the interplay between dynamics and geometry.

faculty.name: Richard Kenyon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies dimer models, random surfaces, and connections between combinatorics, statistical mechanics, and discrete probability.

faculty.name: Ivan Loseu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on geometric representation theory, quantizations of symplectic singularities, and category O for Cherednik algebras.

faculty.name: Yair Minsky
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the geometry and topology of 3-manifolds via Kleinian groups, Teichmuller theory, and geometric group theory; co-proved the Ending Lamination Conjecture.

faculty.name: Andrew Neitzke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates supersymmetric quantum field theories and their connections to hyperkahler geometry, mirror symmetry, and enumerative invariants.

faculty.name: Hee Oh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies homogeneous dynamics and discrete subgroups of Lie groups with applications to number theory, including Apollonian circle packings and measure rigidity.

faculty.name: Alexander Petrov
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on arithmetic geometry and number theory with connections to the Langlands program and p-adic geometry.

faculty.name: Sam Raskin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the proof of the geometric Langlands conjecture using derived algebraic geometry, D-modules, and representation-theoretic methods; New Horizons Prize recipient.

faculty.name: Vladimir Rokhlin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Inventor of the fast multipole method; develops fast numerical algorithms for elliptic PDEs, scattering theory, and integral equations.

faculty.name: Wilhelm Schlag
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies nonlinear dispersive PDEs and spectral theory using methods from multilinear harmonic analysis and paradifferential calculus.

faculty.name: John Schotland
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical theory for inverse scattering problems with applications to optical imaging, quantum optics, and wave propagation in random media.

faculty.name: Junliang Shen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on enumerative geometry and its connections with topology and mathematical physics, studying moduli spaces and intersection theory.

faculty.name: Charles Smart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies elliptic PDE, random Schrodinger operators, and homogenization theory bridging analysis, probability, and mathematical physics.

faculty.name: Dan Spielman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer of smoothed analysis of algorithms; applies spectral graph theory and combinatorial methods to algorithm design, coding theory, and complex networks.

faculty.name: Van Vu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops probabilistic and combinatorial methods for problems in additive number theory, random matrix theory, and data science.

faculty.name: Lu Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geometric analysis, differential geometry, and regularity of minimal surfaces and geometric flows.

faculty.name: John Wettlaufer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies mathematical methods to geophysical and climate problems including phase transitions, fluid dynamics, and ice physics.
